name: All Blue
image_alt: Deep blue-purple potato with blue flesh sliced in half
description: A striking heirloom potato with deep blue-purple skin and vivid blue flesh throughout. Waxy texture with a slightly nutty, earthy flavor. The anthocyanin pigment that gives it color is a powerful antioxidant — one of the most nutritious potato varieties available.
history: A South American heirloom with roots in the ancient Andean potato tradition where blue, purple, and black potatoes have been cultivated for thousands of years. Introduced to North American gardeners through heirloom seed networks in the 1980s.
seasonal_quirks: Mid-season producer that performs well in cool climates. The blue pigment is water-soluble — boiling causes significant color loss. Roasting or baking preserves the dramatic color far better.
harvest_cues: Vines die back at maturity — dig carefully to avoid puncturing the thin skins. Tubers are medium-sized and somewhat irregular in shape. Cure for 1 week before storage.
variety_notes: The blue color persists best when roasted at high heat or used in cold potato salads with acid-based dressings — acid helps stabilize anthocyanins. Sliced thin and roasted into chips, the color is extraordinary.
preservation: Store in cool, dark, humid conditions. Keeps 3–4 months. Color fades over long storage.
